title: Spillovers and Co-movements among Gold, Crude Oil, and Stock Markets: A Bibliometric Analysis and Science Mapping (2010–2025)
author: Collantes Palomino, Hugo Yvan
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Data, code, and outputs to reproduce the bibliometric analysis and science mapping reported in the associated article. The corpus comprises 1,081 unique documents (2010–2026) retrieved from the Web of Science Core Collection (SCI-EXPANDED, SSCI, ESCI) and Scopus on 27 January 2026, following PRISMA 2020. 
 The package includes: - the bibliometrix corpus as both an RData "M" object (1,081 × 71) and CSV; - a deterministic Python pipeline (PYTHONHASHSEED=0, sorted node/edge insertion) that regenerates every reported network statistic byte-for-byte; - four network analyses: keyword co-occurrence, author co-citation, bibliographic coupling (2022–2025), and country co-authorship; - robustness checks: a 9-configuration Louvain resolution × seed grid (mean NMI vs. baseline = 0.799) and a Leiden cross-check (Q = 0.238; NMI = 0.740); - the keyword lemmatization dictionary and supplementary summary files; - all figures at 300 dpi, plus the PRISMA 2020 flow diagram. 
 To reproduce: install code/requirements.txt (Python 3.12.3) and run code/run_network_analysis.py, which rewrites the supplementary/ files. Released under CC-BY 4.0.
steps_raw:
Data, code, and outputs to reproduce the bibliometric analysis and science mapping reported in the associated article. The corpus comprises 1,081 unique documents (2010–2026) retrieved from the Web of Science Core Collection (SCI-EXPANDED, SSCI, ESCI) and Scopus on 27 January 2026, following PRISMA 2020. 
 The package includes: - the bibliometrix corpus as both an RData "M" object (1,081 × 71) and CSV; - a deterministic Python pipeline (PYTHONHASHSEED=0, sorted node/edge insertion) that regenerates every reported network statistic byte-for-byte; - four network analyses: keyword co-occurrence, author co-citation, bibliographic coupling (2022–2025), and country co-authorship; - robustness checks: a 9-configuration Louvain resolution × seed grid (mean NMI vs. baseline = 0.799) and a Leiden cross-check (Q = 0.238; NMI = 0.740); - the keyword lemmatization dictionary and supplementary summary files; - all figures at 300 dpi, plus the PRISMA 2020 flow diagram. 
 To reproduce: install code/requirements.txt (Python 3.12.3) and run code/run_network_analysis.py, which rewrites the supplementary/ files. Released under CC-BY 4.0.
